message:
fix(core): prevent strxfrm null-terminator write when buffer is exactly filled

When src_len >= limit, the previous code wrote a null terminator at
dest[copy_len] which could be at position limit-1 after copying only
limit-1 bytes, wasting a byte. Worse, when limit equals dest.len(),
the unconditional null write was correct but the copy was short by one.
Now copy up to limit bytes and only null-terminate if space remains,
matching POSIX strxfrm semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
